title: Coconut wax candle -- lime and bergamot
subtitle: A bright citrus container candle made with coconut wax for a smooth, creamy finish
excerpt: Coconut wax produces the smoothest, creamiest wax surface of any natural wax and holds fragrance well. Lime and bergamot are both top-note citrus oils; used together at low concentration they hold reasonably well in coconut wax poured at a lower temperature than soy.
sourceNotes: Coconut wax properties and pour temperature: candle supply manufacturer notes. Citrus essential oil retention in lower-melt-point waxes: candle formulation reference.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure 48 hours for best scent throw.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Candles keep two years. Discard if the wax surface turns oily or the citrus scent has gone flat.

glossaryTerms.definition: Hydrogenated coconut oil wax for container candles. Melt point 45 to 55°C. Sets to a very smooth, creamy-white finish. Holds 6 to 8% fragrance. Add fragrance at 60°C and pour at 55°C.

content.text: Coconut wax has the smoothest finish of any natural wax -- it sets without the slight frosting that soy can develop. Its lower melt point also means a lower pour temperature, which is better for citrus essential oils.
holds lime and bergamot better than a higher-temperature wax because less evaporates during the pour.

items.prepNote: about 3.5% of wax weight; about 100 drops
about 3% of wax weight; about 80 drops
CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm jar

content.text: Stick the wick tab to the jar base. Melt 140 g of coconut wax in the pouring pitcher over a double boiler to 65 to 70°C -- coconut wax melts faster than soy. Remove from the heat.

Cool the wax to 60°C. Add the lime and bergamot essential oils by weight and stir for 30 seconds. Pour at 55°C. The lower pour temperature helps preserve the citrus top notes. Fill to 1.5 cm below the rim. Leave undisturbed for two hours.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. Cure for 48 hours. The cold throw is fresh and sharp; hot throw is lighter than a fragrance-oil candle but noticeable and clean.

Allow the full first burn to reach the jar edges. Trim wick before each subsequent burn. Citrus essential oil candles have a lighter hot throw than fragrance oil candles -- this is expected and normal.
